content: Epilepsy is defined as ≥2 unprovoked seizures >24 hours apart, or one unprovoked seizure with ≥60% recurrence risk over 10 years. Seizures are classified by onset: focal (aware or impaired awareness, originating in one hemisphere), generalized (bilateral networks, including absence, tonic-clonic, myoclonic, atonic), or unknown onset. Focal seizures may secondarily generalize. Epilepsy syndromes include temporal lobe epilepsy (most common focal), juvenile myoclonic epilepsy (JME), childhood absence epilepsy, and Lennox-Gastaut syndrome. Status epilepticus is continuous seizure >5 minutes or recurrent seizures without recovery. Epileptogenesis involves imbalance between excitatory (glutamate) and inhibitory (GABA) neurotransmission, ion channel dysfunction, and network reorganization.
